title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Nail Fungus
intro: Herbal teas for nail fungus are natural remedies that are used to treat fungal infections of the nails, particularly those caused by fungi such as dermatophytes and yeasts. These teas can be effective in treating nail fungus because they contain antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help to combat the infection and promote healthy nail growth. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat nail fungus include tea tree oil tea, which has antifungal properties that help to combat fungal infections; peppermint tea, which helps to improve blood circulation and promote healthy nail growth; chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling; and garlic tea, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth. Additionally, other herbal teas such as oregano tea, thyme tea, and lavender tea can also be used to treat nail fungus due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus, a common fungal infection affecting the nails, due to their antifungal properties. The tea's antifungal properties help to inhibit the growth of fungal cells, thereby reducing the severity of the infection. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus, such as eucalyptol and cineole, have been found to exhibit antifungal activity, making it an effective natural remedy for nail fungus. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas may help to treat nail fungus by promoting healthy nail growth, reducing inflammation, and preventing further fungal infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 1/4 cup of Eucalyptus globulus leaves to the cooled water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool to room temperature.
Soak your affected nail in the cooled tea for 30 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, hives, or itching, as well as digestive issues like nausea, diarrhea, or stomach cramps. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, as well as to carefully monitor your body's response to the treatment.
preparation_usage: Soak affected nails in 1 cup of warm Eucalyptus globulus tea for 30 minutes.
Drink 1/2 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day for 2-3 months.
Use Eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a carrier oil to massage the affected nails.
Apply a few drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the affected nails daily.
Repeat steps 1-4 consistently for 6-8 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil tea, have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus ailment due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The active constituents, such as terpinen-4-ol and cineole, help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, promoting a healthy nail environment. The bioactive compounds in Melaleuca alternifolia teas work by penetrating the nail plate and nail bed, reducing fungal infection and inflammation, and ultimately leading to the recovery of healthy nails. Regular consumption of Melaleuca alternifolia teas can offer benefits such as faster nail growth, improved nail texture, and a reduced risk of fungal infections, making it a popular natural remedy for treating nail fungus ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 2 tablespoons of dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, and a tea strainer or infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the Melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the strainer or infuser.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for best results, ideally after meals and before bedtime.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including avoiding application to broken skin, using it in moderation, and monitoring for signs of excessive sensitivity or intolerance.
preparation_usage: Soak affected nails in 2 cups of warm Melaleuca alternifolia tea for 30 minutes.
Repeat soaking 2-3 times a week for 3-6 months.
Apply 2-3 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to affected nails.
Massage oil into nail and surrounding skin 2-3 times a week.
Monitor progress and adjust treatment as needed, consulting a doctor if necessary.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including nail fungus, due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, help to combat fungal infections by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and promoting a healthy immune response. Drinking Echinacea purpurea teas may help to treat nail fungus by reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and enhancing the body's natural defenses against fungal infections. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat nail fungus include reduced symptoms, faster recovery, and a lower risk of recurrence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Echinacea flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 2-3 weeks for nail fungus treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues, particularly when consumed in large quantities or over an extended period. Precautions include monitoring for signs of interaction with other medications, such as blood thinners, and being cautious when using Echinacea purpurea teas in individuals with weakened immune systems or those undergoing chemotherapy.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2 times a day.
Consume 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea root daily.
Maintain a consistent usage for 6-8 weeks minimum.
Monitor nail fungus progress after 4-6 weeks of usage.
Combine Echinacea with other natural treatments for enhanced results.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the sage plant, have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents, including thujone, camphor, and borneol, possess potent antifungal activity that helps to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, thereby promoting a healthy nail environment. The use of Salvia officinalis teas in treating nail fungus has been associated with benefits such as reduced fungal infections, improved nail appearance, and enhanced overall nail health. By incorporating Salvia officinalis teas into one's treatment regimen, individuals may experience a reduction in symptoms and improved outcomes in managing nail fungus.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the boiling water to help soothe and calm the nail area.
Steep the Salvia officinalis leaves in the water for 5-7 minutes to release the active ingredients.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool down to room temperature.
Soak your affected nail in the cooled tea for 30 minutes, 2-3 times a week, to help treat nail fungus.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions such as skin irritation or anaphylaxis. When using Salvia officinalis teas to treat nail fungus, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which can lead to interactions with other medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions, and to monitor for signs of adverse reactions, especially when consuming the tea in large quantities or for extended periods.
preparation_usage: Soak affected nails in 1 cup of Salvia officinalis tea for 30 minutes daily.
Drink 2 cups of Salvia officinalis tea 3 times a day for 2 months.
Add 2 tablespoons of Salvia officinalis powder to your bath water daily.
Massage 2 tablespoons of Salvia officinalis oil into affected nails 2 times a day.
Repeat treatment for 3 months for best results, then maintain with 1 treatment per week.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, made from the leaves of the lemongrass plant, have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as citral and geraniol, help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, thereby promoting healthy nail growth and reducing symptoms of the condition. These herbal preparations have been found to be effective in treating nail fungus by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and by modulating the immune system to fight off the fungal infection. The benefits of using Cymbopogon citratus teas to treat nail fungus include reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, fewer side effects, and a more natural approach to healing, making it an attractive alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Cymbopogon citratus leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of honey (optional)
Rinse Cymbopogon citratus leaves and chop them into small pieces
Steep the chopped leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes
Strain the liquid and discard the leaves, then add honey if desired
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, ideally on an empty stomach, for best results
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Comptes rendus biologies", Cymbopogon citratus teas may have antifungal properties, as studies have shown its aqueous extract to be effective in controlling fungal diseases such as grey mould on tomato fruit, suggesting potential benefits for nail fungus treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be effective in treating nail fungus, but may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications, especially blood thinners or certain antibiotics. To use Cymbopogon citratus teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and be cautious when using it with other herbal remedies or supplements, as it may enhance or diminish their effects.
preparation_usage: Soak your affected nail in 1 cup of warm water for 10 minutes.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Cymbopogon citratus in 1 cup of hot water for 5 minutes.
Cool the tea, then soak your nail for 30 minutes, 3 times a week.
Drink 1 cup of the tea daily to help prevent fungal infections.
Continue using Cymbopogon citratus tea for 6-12 months to see results.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas, also known as wormwood tea, have been used in traditional medicine to treat nail fungus ailment due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, such as thujone, artabsin, and flavonoids, help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and promote a healthy nail environment. By reducing inflammation and fighting fungal infections, Artemisia absinthium teas can help to alleviate symptoms of nail fungus, such as discoloration, thickening, and brittleness. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat nail fungus include a natural and non-invasive approach, reduced risk of side effects, and potential improvement in nail health over time.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help treat nail fungus.
Repeat the process for 2-3 months to see noticeable results.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but its use may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, potentially exacerbating the condition. When using Artemisia absinthium teas to treat nail fungus, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, avoiding prolonged use, and monitoring liver function, as the tea may cause liver damage in rare cases.
preparation_usage: Soak affected nails in 1 cup of warm water with 2 tablespoons of Artemisia absinthium tea.
Soak for 30 minutes, 2 times a day, for 2-3 weeks.
Apply a few drops of Artemisia absinthium tea directly to affected nails.
Leave the solution on for 30 minutes to an hour before washing off.
Repeat application 2 times a day for 2-3 months.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including nail fungus, due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms, thereby promoting a healthy nail environment. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia teas, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, exhibit antifungal activity that helps to combat fungal infections. By consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas, individuals can benefit from a potential reduction in symptoms associated with nail fungus, including discoloration and brittleness, promoting overall nail health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for up to 2 weeks to help treat nail fungus.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat nail fungus, however, they may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners and diabetes medications, which can worsen the condition of people with diabetes. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it is essential to start with small doses and gradually increase the amount, avoid using them during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and be cautious when combining them with other herbal remedies or medications to minimize potential risks.
preparation_usage: Soak affected nails in 1 cup of warm Lavandula angustifolia tea for 30 minutes daily.
Apply 2-3 drops of tea directly to the affected nail area, 3 times a day.
Massage tea into the nail gently for 2-3 minutes, 3 times a day.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia in 1 cup of boiling water as a foot soak, daily.
Continue treatment for 6-8 weeks, observing nail growth and fungus reduction.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the herb rosemary, have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to combat fungal infections by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and reducing inflammation in the affected area, thereby promoting healthy nail growth. The bioactive constituents present in rosemary, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been found to exhibit antifungal activity, making them effective in treating nail fungus. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat nail fungus include reduced symptoms, improved nail appearance, and a decrease in the risk of further infection.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day for nail fungus treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of infection in developing countries, Rosmarinus officinalis teas have not been specifically studied for nail fungus, but the plant extracts showed antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities against various opportunistic microorganisms including Candida albicans, which is often associated with nail fungus.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat nail fungus, including avoiding consumption in large quantities, pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with sensitive stomachs, to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been studied for their potential in treating nail fungus, a common ailment characterized by fungal infections of the nails. The antifungal properties of Curcuma longa teas, attributed to bioactive constituents such as curcumin and turmerone, help to inhibit the growth of fungi and promote a healthy nail environment. These properties, combined with the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects of Curcuma longa teas, contribute to their ability to treat nail fungus by reducing fungal infections and promoting the growth of healthy nail tissue. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat nail fungus include reduced symptoms, improved nail appearance, and a natural, non-invasive treatment option with minimal side effects.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Curcuma longa root powder.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of Curcuma longa root powder to the water and stir well.
Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the Curcuma longa to infuse.
Strain the liquid and drink 1 cup of the Curcuma longa tea 2-3 times a day to help treat nail fungus.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Curcuma longa teas for nail fungus are not mentioned in the given study, but Curcuma longa is one of the medicinal herbs reviewed for atopic dermatitis management due to its anti-inflammatory, anti-microbial, antioxidant, and wound recovery properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used to treat nail fungus ailment, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Curcuma longa teas to treat this ailment, precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with other medications, and to monitor for signs of liver damage, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, and to use the tea in moderation and with caution, especially in people with pre-existing medical conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents present in thyme tea, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause nail fungus, thereby promoting healthy nail growth. By consuming thyme tea, individuals can benefit from its antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to combat the infection and prevent its spread. Regular consumption of thyme tea may also help to alleviate symptoms associated with nail fungus, such as discoloration and brittleness, and promote a healthier nail environment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Steep the dried Thymus vulgaris leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 2-3 weeks for nail fungus treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of biological regulators and homeostatic agents, Thymus vulgaris teas for nail fungus may exhibit potential due to the significant antibacterial activity of its 80% methanolic fraction against Bacillus subtilis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, hives, or itching, as well as gastrointestinal issues like nausea or diarrhea. Precautions to take when using Thymus vulgaris teas for this treatment include avoiding its use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as individuals with sensitive stomachs, and being cautious not to ingest excessive amounts, as this may cause dizziness or headaches.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have gained attention as a potential natural remedy for treating nail fungus, a common and often difficult-to-treat ailment. The anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of Aloe barbadensis teas help to reduce inflammation and combat fungal growth, making it easier to treat the underlying cause of the condition. Bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan present in Aloe barbadensis teas are responsible for its antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and promote healthy nail growth. Drinking Aloe barbadensis teas regularly can provide benefits in treating nail fungus, including reduced symptoms, improved nail health, and a lower risk of infection recurrence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 1 cup of water, and a strainer.
Mix the Aloe gel and water in a saucepan and heat it over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a strainer into a cup to remove any solid particles.
Drink 1/4 cup of the Aloe tea 3 times a day for nail fungus treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of International Society of Preventive & Community Dentistry, Aloe barbadensis teas for nail fungus are not mentioned in the provided study as it focuses on the use of medicinal herbs in oral health care, specifically for preventing gingival and periodontal diseases.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea or stomach cramps. Precautions to take when using Aloe barbadensis teas include starting with small amounts, avoiding contact with the eyes and sensitive areas, and being cautious if you have any existing conditions such as kidney or liver disease.

The benefits of using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat nail fungus include promoting healthy nail growth, reducing inflammation and discomfort, and preventing the spread of fungal infection.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used as a natural remedy to treat nail fungus ailments due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, possess antifungal activity, which helps to inhibit the growth of fungi and prevent the spread of infection. Drinking Ginkgo biloba teas regularly may help to improve nail health by increasing blood flow to the nail bed, reducing inflammation, and promoting the growth of healthy nail tissue. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat nail fungus include reduced fungal growth, improved nail appearance, and a lower risk of infection recurrence.

Add the dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the tea infuser or the heat-resistant cup.
Pour the cooled water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help treat nail fungus.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, derived from the leaves of the wild marjoram plant, have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus ailments due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents, such as carvacrol and thymol, help to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms, thereby reducing the severity of the infection. By promoting a healthy fungal balance and reducing inflammation, Origanum vulgare teas can help to clear up fungal infections of the nails, leading to a faster recovery and healthier nails. The benefits of using Origanum vulgare teas to treat nail fungus include a natural and non-invasive approach, reduced risk of side effects, and improved overall nail health.

Strain the tea and discard the leaves.

preparations.preparation_study: According to Scientific reports, Origanum vulgare teas for nail fungus may be effective due to its essential oil's antimicrobial properties, particularly its activity against certain bacteria such as Escherichia coli and Klebsiella pneumoniae at low concentrations of 2% and 2% respectively.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can potentially cause allergic reactions, nausea, and stomach upset when consumed to treat nail fungus, as the body may not be accustomed to the high concentration of essential oils contained within the herb. To minimize side effects when using Origanum vulgare teas for this purpose, it is recommended to start with small doses, gradually increase the amount as needed, and discontinue use if any severe or persistent side effects occur.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus ailment due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of fungal cells, thus promoting a healthy nail environment. Aloe vera contains bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, which exhibit antifungal activity and help to combat fungal infections. The benefits of using aloe vera teas to treat nail fungus ailment include reduced risk of infection, accelerated healing, and a natural, non-toxic approach to managing this condition.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water and 2 tablespoons of Aloe vera gel.
Mix Aloe vera gel and boiling water in a bowl.
Let the mixture cool down for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup to remove any pulp.
Drink the Aloe vera tea 2-3 times a day for nail fungus treatment.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Infectious disorders drug targets, Aloe vera teas for nail fungus are not directly mentioned in the provided study, however, the study does mention Aloe vera as a major therapeutically active natural herbal resource for the treatment of dermatological infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can be effective in treating nail fungus, but some possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications due to its potential anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties. When using Aloe vera teas to treat nail fungus, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with small amounts, monitoring blood sugar levels if you have diabetes, and avoiding consumption if you have sensitive stomachs or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Allium sativum teas, also known as garlic tea, have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus ailments due to their antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents present in Allium sativum teas, such as allicin and diallyl disulfide, help to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail infections. These compounds exhibit potent antifungal activity, thereby helping to treat nail fungus by reducing fungal colonization and promoting healthy nail growth. The benefits of using Allium sativum teas to treat nail fungus include reduced inflammation, improved nail appearance, and a lower risk of infection recurrence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 2-3 cloves of Allium sativum (garlic), and a tea infuser.
Peel the garlic cloves and put them in the tea infuser.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Steep the garlic in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then remove the tea infuser.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day, to help treat nail fungus.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Allium sativum teas may not be directly related to nail fungus, but it was found to inhibit the growth of pathogens isolated from urinary tract infection.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Allium sativum teas can potentially cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, which may worsen the nail fungus condition or lead to other complications. To minimize risks, it is recommended to start with low concentrations, monitor the body's response closely, and avoid using Allium sativum teas in combination with other treatments or medications, especially blood thinners and diabetes medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus ailments due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, exhibit potent antifungal activities, inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens and alleviating symptoms of nail fungus. Regular consumption of Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas has been found to help treat nail fungus by reducing the severity of fungal infections, improving nail appearance, and preventing further fungal growth. The benefits of using Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas for treating nail fungus include their natural and non-invasive nature, making them a suitable alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 2 tablespoons of Cinnamomum zeylanicum powder to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool.
Soak your affected nails in the tea for 30 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Medical mycology, Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas for nail fungus have demonstrated significant antifungal activities against Trichophyton mentagrophytes and Microsporum canis, with a geometric mean (GM) MIC of 0.058 to 3.73 mg/ml and GM (MFC) ranging from 0.058 to 7.46 mg/ml, indicating a potential use as a natural treatment option for nail fungus.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas can be used to treat nail fungus, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or digestive issues. When using Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas to treat nail fungus, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with a small dose, avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, and monitoring blood sugar levels if you have diabetes, as excessive cinnamon consumption may cause blood sugar fluctuations.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus radiata teas have been used as a natural remedy to treat nail fungus, a common fungal infection that affects the nails and surrounding skin. The antifungal and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to combat the fungal infection, promoting a healthy nail growth and preventing further infection. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus radiata, including eucalyptol, limonene, and pinene, contribute to its antifungal and antimicrobial activity, making it an effective treatment for nail fungus. By using Eucalyptus radiata teas, individuals can benefit from a natural and non-invasive treatment that not only helps to clear the fungal infection but also promotes nail health and prevents future occurrences.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus radiata leaves.
Boil the water in a pot and add the Eucalyptus radiata leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves. Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste.
Drink the tea once a day, on an empty stomach. Repeat for 6-8 weeks for best results.
Use a cotton swab to apply the tea to affected nails, 2-3 times a day. Cover with a bandage to enhance absorption.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus radiata teas can be used as a natural remedy for treating nail fungus, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and nausea in some individuals. To use Eucalyptus radiata teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid taking it for extended periods, and to monitor your body's response closely, as it may interact with other medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Hyssopus officinalis teas, also known as hyssop tea, have been traditionally used to treat nail fungus ailments due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and promote a healthy nail environment. By drinking hyssop tea, individuals can benefit from its ability to reduce inflammation, improve nail circulation, and enhance the overall immune response to fight off fungal infections. The benefits of using hyssop tea to treat nail fungus include a decrease in symptoms, improved nail health, and a reduced risk of further infection.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Hyssopus officinalis leaves.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature. You can speed up the process by placing the tea in the refrigerator.
Soak your affected nail in the cooled tea for 20-30 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. Discard any remaining tea after the recommended time.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hyssopus officinalis teas can cause mild gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, stomach cramps, and diarrhea when used to treat nail fungus, and it may also interact with certain medications or worsen underlying health conditions. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response closely, and avoid using Hyssopus officinalis teas if you have allergies, are pregnant, or breastfeeding.

preparation_study: According to Indian journal of dental research : official publication of Indian Society for Dental Research, Eucalyptus globulus teas do not have any information in the given study regarding nail fungus, but the study does show that Eucalyptus galbie (not Eucalyptus globulus) extracts exhibited the highest antimicrobial activity against E. faecalis.
intro_study: According to "The Journal of family practice", teas for nail fungus, such as Melaleuca alternifolia (tea tree) oil, showed comparable efficacy to 1% clotrimazole solution in treating toenail onychomycosis, with partial or full resolution in 60% of patients after 6 months.
